assets.name: lecture_04_figure_01.png
assets.rationale: All four candidates are title-card frames showing only the Stanford University opening slate. None contains blackboard mathematics, diagrams, lecturer-board layout, or any note-useful visual evidence.

assets.name: lecture_04_figure_05.png
assets.rationale: This frame gives the clearest and most complete view of the board diagrams in this window. The large circular outline is mostly visible, the irregular closed curve on the right is fully present, and the lecturer blocks less of the drawing than in candidate 1. Candidate 3 loses too much of the right-hand diagram, while candidate 4 shifts away from these shapes and shows only a fragmentary equation board.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: The F equals mA equation does have second time derivatives.
